jokes.text: 1939? Oh, my God! I've gone back in time! I've got to warn everybody about Hitler! And get to the ice-cream store!
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he's time-traveled to 1939 after hearing a radio show
jokes.punchline: Equal priority given to warning about Hitler and getting ice cream
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating world-historical catastrophe and personal desire with equal weight. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Shut up! You haven't even been born yet!
jokes.setup: Homer still believes he's in 1939
jokes.punchline: Applies time travel logic to dismiss Barney
jokes.explanation: Homer's commitment to the delusion creates absurd logic. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: That was Men Without Hats. Or as they're known today, Men Without Jobs.
jokes.setup: DJ introducing 80s band Men Without Hats
jokes.punchline: Joke about band's career decline using similar phrasing
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but fairly predictable celebrity mockery. Good structure but low character integration.

jokes.text: We're broadcasting live from P.J. O'H.'s and this place makes Animal House look like Terms of Endearment.
jokes.setup: DJ describing wild bar atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic comparison using contrasting movie references
jokes.explanation: Solid comparison joke with clear cultural references. Decent structure but generic radio DJ delivery.

jokes.text: That's no way to address Sir Drinks-a-Lot.
jokes.setup: Marge telling Homer to move from blocking traffic
jokes.punchline: Homer gives himself a mock-noble drinking title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-aggrandizement about his drinking problem. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Oh, quit complaining. It's half the work of a divorced dad.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about monthly father-child day
jokes.punchline: Homer reduces parenting to comparative labor math
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant Homer logic about minimal parenting effort. Excellent economy and character-specific worldview.

jokes.text: Yeah, but it's twice as much as a deadbeat dad.
jokes.setup: Homer's divorced dad comparison
jokes.punchline: Continues the math but goes even darker with deadbeat dad reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke, getting progressively darker about bad parenting standards.

jokes.text: They know I'm kidding.
jokes.setup: Marge's shock at Homer's deadbeat dad comment
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious assumption that he was obviously joking
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness. Good character moment but not exceptional as a standalone joke.

jokes.text: If it doesn't have Siamese twins in a jar, it's not a fair.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting a book fair
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre carnival sideshow standard for 'fair'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd Homer logic. The 'Siamese twins in a jar' detail is perfectly grotesque and memorable.

jokes.text: What? And Marmaduke doesn't? [make history come alive]
jokes.setup: Lisa saying the Smithsonian exhibit makes history come alive
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically defending Marmaduke comic strip as historically significant
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic defense of lowbrow culture. Homer's wounded pride over Marmaduke is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Uncle Sam needs to spend our tax dollars on the essentials: antitobacco programs, pro-tobacco programs, killing wild donkeys and Israel.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning corporate sponsorship of Smithsonian
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre list of government spending priorities
jokes.explanation: Absurd government spending satire with contradictory programs and random specificity. The 'wild donkeys' detail is wonderfully arbitrary.

jokes.text: Thank God for grave robbers.
jokes.setup: Lisa gasping at Lincoln's hat in the exhibit
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact gratitude for grave robbing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moral blindness and economy. Four words that reveal his complete lack of reverence for historical sanctity.

jokes.text: Who's Fonzie? Don't they teach you anything in school? He freed the squares.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking who Fonzie is
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-historical description of Fonzie's cultural impact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic treating TV character as historical figure. 'He freed the squares' is perfect pseudo-profound language.

jokes.text: You just licked off the part that forbids cruel and unusual punishment.
jokes.setup: Homer getting chocolate on the Bill of Rights
jokes.punchline: Specific constitutional protection that was damaged, setting up implied threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic setup for what's about to happen to Homer. The specific amendment choice creates perfect dark comedy.

jokes.text: Finally a company that understands my needs.
jokes.setup: Omnitouch saying real money doesn't grow on trees
jokes.punchline: Homer grateful that a corporation understands his financial limitations
jokes.explanation: Homer's naive gratitude toward obvious corporate manipulation. Good character moment but predictable.

jokes.text: We sure do. It's called caring.
jokes.setup: Homer saying company understands his needs
jokes.punchline: Corporate buzzword disguising exploitation as compassion
jokes.explanation: Standard corporate speak satire. Functional but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: We prefer to think of them as 'keep in touch' towers.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about cellular transmitter on roof
jokes.punchline: Corporate euphemism for intrusive technology
jokes.explanation: Decent corporate euphemism joke but fairly predictable. The wordplay is functional but not clever.

jokes.text: That Andy Capp was onto something.
jokes.setup: Homer finding the living room couch comfortable
jokes.punchline: Reference to comic strip character who sleeps on furniture
jokes.explanation: Good Homer reference to obscure comic character. Shows his identification with lazy cartoon characters.

jokes.text: Just pretend it's a tree, honey. Or as we say at Omnitouch, a progress tree.
jokes.setup: Marge calling the antenna an eyesore
jokes.punchline: Corporate rebranding of ugly infrastructure as environmental metaphor
jokes.explanation: Solid corporate doublespeak satire. 'Progress tree' is a good specific example of meaningless branding.

jokes.text: You gave away my room?
jokes.setup: Homer explaining they needed space for electronic equipment
jokes.punchline: Lisa's shocked realization of what happened
jokes.explanation: Standard shock reaction to absurd family decision. Works as plot development but not exceptionally funny.

jokes.text: Funny how your mind works in a crisis.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why he didn't put equipment in his own room
jokes.punchline: Homer's faux-philosophical observation about his own thoughtlessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness without actual insight. Great economy in expressing his backwards thinking.

jokes.text: I thought I'd never have to say that again.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining situation as 'Daddy's desecration of priceless artifact'
jokes.punchline: Implies Homer has desecrated priceless artifacts before
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback implying a pattern of Homer's cultural vandalism. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: That's right, The Odd Couple! So meet your comically mismatched roommate, Bart Simpson!
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning her favorite movie was taped over
jokes.punchline: Homer incorrectly remembering movie and applying sitcom logic to real situation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about sitcom conventions and Homer's pop culture confusion. Nice self-awareness of the show's own format.

jokes.text: I'm gonna make your life a living hell.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa becoming roommates
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate threat delivered to TV theme music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart threat with good timing against the sitcom music. Predictable but well-executed character moment.

jokes.text: One: I am Bart, thy god. If I am out, the Krusty doll is thy god. If the doll's with me, you will worship the night-light.
jokes.setup: Bart establishing roommate rules
jokes.punchline: Elaborate hierarchy of worship objects in biblical language
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart megalomania with biblical parody structure. The detailed succession plan is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Great. I'm gonna eat mayonnaise.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to call Omnitouch about privacy issues
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely unrelated announcement about condiment consumption
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Homer non sequitur. The specificity of mayonnaise and casual delivery make it memorable.

jokes.text: Hey, that's what I do in my room, Lise. I squeak my chair.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about Bart squeaking his chair
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact defense of annoying behavior as routine
jokes.explanation: Good Bart logic treating annoying behavior as reasonable personal routine. Solid character consistency.

jokes.text: Bart was winning. Then he said, 'This is stupid.' And he stopped, and I won!
jokes.setup: Homer excitedly describing a pen-clicking race
jokes.punchline: Bart's self-awareness defeating Homer's childish enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Excellent character dynamics - Bart's maturity defeating Homer's immaturity. Perfect illustration of their relationship.

jokes.text: That wasn't your mayonnaise, was it?
jokes.setup: Lisa being mad at Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's concern that he ate someone else's mayonnaise
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier non sequitur. Homer's misunderstanding of Lisa's anger is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: No, it's not. [in response to 'It is fun to me']
jokes.setup: Lisa saying studying is fun to her
jokes.punchline: Homer's flat denial of Lisa's personal preference
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inability to accept others' different preferences. Good character moment but simple execution.

jokes.text: Hey, it's Krusty. I need you to get me out of another jam. I picked up this chick last night. At least I thought she was a chick.
jokes.setup: Krusty calling for help via intercepted phone call
jokes.punchline: Revelation about mistaken gender identity in hookup
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty character moment showing his seedy lifestyle. Solid setup and reveal but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Somebody wants Mommy to change baby's diaper! Is that somebody you, Homer?
jokes.setup: Lisa having stomach problems
jokes.punchline: Marge assuming Homer needs diaper change based on his behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Marge sarcasm about Homer's childishness. Nice parallel between Homer and actual baby.

jokes.text: You know, before I learned to chuckle mindlessly, I was headed for an early grave myself.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert explaining laughter as medicine
jokes.punchline: Admission that his constant chuckling is mindless coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character deconstruction. Dr. Hibbert's self-awareness about his annoying trait while still doing it is perfect dark comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, no, you don't! Screw that touchy, queer-y crap! We'll take the harsh antacids.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting herbal remedies
jokes.punchline: Homer's aggressive rejection of alternative medicine
jokes.explanation: Good Homer close-mindedness about anything unfamiliar. Character-consistent but dated language affects impact.

jokes.text: I sure settled his hash. Imagine that quack thinking he'd try one of those crazy new age cures.
jokes.setup: Homer rejecting alternative medicine
jokes.punchline: Homer's pride in dismissing actual doctor as quack
jokes.explanation: Good Homer backwards logic calling real doctor a quack. Solid irony about who has medical expertise.

jokes.text: Nah, you must be thinking of your mother. I'm always exploring alternatives and expanding my horizons into realms of-
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Homer thinks new age stuff is stupid
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely false claim about being open-minded
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-delusion about his open-mindedness immediately after proving the opposite. Nice character contradiction.

jokes.text: I'm just saying I can get along in life without a 'toothbrush.'
jokes.setup: Homer claiming acceptance of other cultures
jokes.punchline: Dismissing basic hygiene tool as exotic cultural practice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer misunderstanding of what constitutes 'other cultures.' Perfect character ignorance with great economy.

jokes.text: And a-ooga-booga to you too.
jokes.setup: Store clerk saying 'Namaste'
jokes.punchline: Homer's mock-primitive response to foreign greeting
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent ignorance but relies on cheap stereotyping. Shows Homer's cultural insensitivity but not cleverly.

jokes.text: And what's keeping Joan Rivers alive? Fetal grindings.
jokes.setup: Discussion of various new age remedies
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely dark explanation for celebrity longevity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific celebrity joke. The casual delivery of horrifying content works perfectly.

jokes.text: Can you pee in it?
jokes.setup: Clerk explaining sensory deprivation tank
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate practical concern about tank usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer question cutting through spiritual concepts to bodily functions. Excellent economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Wow. My mind just created that out of nothing.
jokes.setup: Lisa experiencing hallucinations in the sensory deprivation tank
jokes.punchline: Her intellectual analysis of her own psychedelic experience
jokes.explanation: Very Lisa - even during hallucinations, she's analyzing the neurological process rather than just experiencing it.

jokes.text: Uh-oh. A cow. Eat lead, Bossy.
jokes.setup: Bart doing fake criminal radio drama
jokes.punchline: Treating cow encounter as deadly threat requiring gunfire
jokes.explanation: Good Bart improvisation with absurd criminal logic. 'Eat lead, Bossy' is a memorable line with good contrast.

jokes.text: But if anybody asks, you hit him.
jokes.setup: Marge hitting Milhouse during prank
jokes.punchline: Marge's suggestion to cover up her violence with Bart's reputation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Marge moment showing hidden ruthlessness. Great economy and character subversion.

jokes.text: The I Ching said I had six months till bankruptcy.
jokes.setup: Store being repossessed
jokes.punchline: New age divination completely wrong about business future
jokes.explanation: Good irony about new age practices failing their practitioners. Solid observational humor about mysticism vs reality.

jokes.text: You should lift with your legs. Yeah, aw, screw it. I got health insurance.
jokes.setup: Mover giving safety advice about lifting
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning safety because of insurance coverage
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about health insurance moral hazard. Solid structure with realistic working-class logic.

jokes.text: Sensory deprivation kicks ass!
jokes.setup: Homer emerging from spiritual experience
jokes.punchline: Reducing transcendent experience to crude enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction to spiritual experience. The crude language contrasting with enlightenment concept works well.

jokes.text: Not so deep the Lord can't find him... and judge him.
jokes.setup: Ned's son praising how deep they buried Homer
jokes.punchline: Ned's theological concern about divine accessibility to buried bodies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders theology applied to practical burial concerns. Dark religious humor with excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, yuck. That sandwich is full of meat! There's bacon. Canadian bacon. Mexican bacon!
jokes.setup: Lisa (in Homer's body) disgusted by meat
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly ridiculous bacon varieties
jokes.explanation: Good body-swap comedy with Lisa's vegetarian values in Homer's meat-loving body. The bacon escalation works well.

jokes.text: Geez, why is Lisa so mad at me? I only came to this dump so I could be with her.
jokes.setup: Homer (in Lisa's body) confused about Lisa's anger
jokes.punchline: Homer calling the ballet a 'dump' while claiming he came for Lisa
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-contradiction about his motivations. The irony of calling ballet a dump while claiming altruism works well.

jokes.text: Boy, I can really be a pain in the butt.
jokes.setup: Lisa experiencing life from Homer's perspective
jokes.punchline: Lisa gains empathy for Homer by literally being him
jokes.explanation: Nice emotional beat in surreal sequence. Lisa's self-awareness while in Homer's body creates empathy.

jokes.text: Boy, I can really be a pain in the butt.
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing his behavior from Lisa's perspective
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of Homer self-awareness about his behavior
jokes.explanation: Rare and effective Homer character growth moment. The body-swap premise creates genuine insight with perfect understatement.

jokes.text: Does it get any better than this? Not to me, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer asks if demolition derby could be better
jokes.punchline: Lisa genuinely agrees, showing their newfound connection
jokes.explanation: Sweet resolution showing Lisa's genuine appreciation for Homer's interests after their experience.
